cityDNA.coreFantasy: A bright showbird harbor capital where public beauty, maritime logistics, and civic reputation are the same system.
cityDNA.readerTakeaway: Auric Plume is not decorative; every color, plume, fan roof, cargo seal, and public display helps the city organize trust.
cityDNA.civilizationThesis: Auric Plume turns spectacle into infrastructure: showbird display, tide-sealed records, cargo yards, public works, star navigation, and harbor ritual all become mechanisms of civic order.
cityDNA.visualIdentity: Feather-fan towers, pearl domes, patchwork awnings, blue civic lamps, bright harbor stone, cargo courts, star instruments, and basilica arches surrounding reflective water.
cityDNA.culturalIdentity: A coastal showbird civilization where visibility is social metadata, reputation is engineered in public, and trade depends on being seen accurately.
cityDNA.symbolicIdentity: Feathers, fans, pearls, patchwork, tide seals, cargo seals, stars, and harbor arches symbolize many visible parts held together by public trust.

canonLocks: Auric Plume is an established coastal showbird capital, not a decorative bird city or temporary port.
The city fuses public display with practical maritime infrastructure.
The Wonder is the Pearl-Feather Roost of the Thousand Fans.
The Beacon is the Patchwork Hall Beacon.
The Seat of Power is the Feather Fan Roofs Tide-Seal Citadel.
The five primary districts are Patchwork Hall, Warehousing / Logistics, Public Works Authority, Observatory / Astronomy, and Harbor Campus.
provisionalCanon: Auric Plume's highest civic virtue is visible trust: citizens prove reliability by making identity, status, cargo, and obligation legible in public.
Public display can become political pressure when reputation control is abused.
rejectedReads: Auric Plume is frivolous or vanity-driven only.
Auric Plume is a generic harbor city with bird ornament added.
The city's bright spectacle should be separated from its logistics or government.
cautionFlags: Do not make showbird identity comic relief.
Do not let color and fan forms become empty decoration; they must communicate rank, trade, trust, or civic role.
Keep cargo, public works, observatory, and harbor ceremony visible enough to prevent the city from collapsing into pure pageantry.

environment.environmentalLogic: The harbor is stage, marketplace, navigation platform, and civic engine. Water movement, visibility, dock access, and public display shape how the city governs itself.

skyline: Pearl-Feather Roost of the Thousand Fans as the dominant wonder-scale horizon
Feather Fan Roofs Tide-Seal Citadel as central authority
Patchwork Hall Beacon as the near/front signal landmark
Harbor Basilica as the ceremonial water-facing landmark
star observatory towers, cargo halls, fan-roofed domes, and bright terraces around blue water

civilization.people: Citizens are showbird beastfolk: vivid, social, posture-aware, reputation-conscious, visually expressive, and comfortable turning public appearance into civic communication.
socialClasses: textile, plume, pearl, and harbor craftspeople
dock laborers, runners, and market sellers
civilization.governance: Government is tide-sealed, public, and performative. Authority depends on visible ceremony, maritime records, harbor permissions, cargo legitimacy, and public interpretation of civic signals.
civilization.religion: Religious expression is moderate and harbor-facing, centered on basilica ceremonies, public vows, maritime mourning, pearl-feather symbolism, and ritual display beside water.
customs: public bargaining is theatrical but legally serious
plume treatment, color, pins, and fabrics announce profession and status
cargo seals and tide seals carry public reputation
festival display doubles as civic verification
harbor ceremonies make arrival and departure socially visible
bodyLanguage: upright posture and deliberate display angles
preference for balconies, ledges, and open plazas
quick recognition of visible rank markers

streets.appearance: Bright, crowded, maritime, and performative: sunlit harbor stone, fan-roof shadows, patchwork awnings, cargo ledgers, permit yards, star instruments, blue civic lamps, and bridgelets over blue water.
streets.movement: Movement follows quays, bridgelets, canal edges, plaza roads, cargo lanes, stair terraces, small boats, and high balcony paths. Harbor schedules, permits, tides, and public ceremonies redirect traffic naturally.
streets.lighting: Daylight is reflective and bright; night is blue-gold with beacon light, basilica windows, observatory instruments, lanterns, and harbor reflections.
streets.weatherEffects: Sea haze, wind, salt glare, sudden harbor clouds, and wet stone affect navigation, banners, crowd mood, and visibility.
streets.dayCycle: Mornings center on cargo unloading, seal checks, public notices, and permit queues; afternoons swell with textile trade, civic errands, and visible display.
streets.nightCycle: Evenings belong to lanterns, harbor food, observatory work, basilica gatherings, blue lamps, and water reflections turning the city into public theater.

housing.wealthy: Pearl-inlaid harbor residences, citadel-adjacent terraces, bright roof gardens, fan-roofed balconies, and windows aimed toward harbor spectacle.
housing.middle: Upper rooms over shops, colorful courtyard apartments, balcony nests, shared roof terraces, patchwork curtains, and small display ledges.
housing.poor: Modest dockside rooms, cargo-adjacent flats, textile-worker courtyards, patched awnings, shared balconies, and cramped but color-conscious harbor housing.

districts.name: Patchwork Hall
districts.civicPurpose: Public assembly, civic notices, social display, beacon watching, and the visible joining of many households, guilds, and trade groups.
districts.visualIdentity: Patchwork awnings, civic plaza fabric, blue lamps, colorful kiosks, fan accents, and public gathering terraces around the Beacon.
districts.emotionalIdentity: Open, social, proud, and theatrical without being unserious.
gameplayAssetNeeds: showbird citizens in public display poses

districts.name: Warehousing / Logistics
districts.civicPurpose: Cargo movement, inventory control, seal verification, dock trust, and maritime bureaucracy.
districts.visualIdentity: Cargo yards, inventory halls, gold-seal monuments, crates, ropes, dock schedules, seal counters, and public ledger boards.
districts.emotionalIdentity: Busy, practical, reputation-heavy, and more serious than the city’s colors first suggest.

districts.name: Public Works Authority
districts.civicPurpose: Harbor repair, route control, dock permits, surveys, municipal measurement, and safe civic infrastructure.
districts.visualIdentity: Survey halls, permit yards, blue lamps, maps, harbor diagrams, measuring poles, repair notices, and engineering plazas.
districts.emotionalIdentity: Measured, bureaucratic, civic-minded, and quietly powerful.

districts.name: Observatory / Astronomy
districts.civicPurpose: Maritime navigation, long-cycle planning, star-chart authority, celestial prediction, and harbor-route legitimacy.
districts.visualIdentity: Star observatory towers, celestial courts, star navigation monuments, blue-gold instruments, charts, lenses, and high terraces above harbor water.
districts.emotionalIdentity: Elevated, precise, contemplative, and politically useful.

districts.name: Harbor Campus
districts.civicPurpose: Harbor ceremony, public vows, arrivals, departures, maritime mourning, and sacred-facing civic identity.
districts.visualIdentity: Harbor Basilica, pearl-like domes, Harbor Arch, Harbor Bridgelet, water-facing plazas, wet stone, banners, and ceremonial approaches.

wonder.name: Pearl-Feather Roost of the Thousand Fans
wonder.symbolicRole: Collective display made stable, navigable, magnificent, and civic.
wonder.visualRole: Dominant pearl-and-feather skyline structure with enormous fan forms, roost-like terraces, bright domes, gold trim, and harbor-facing spectacle.
wonder.civicRole: The public emblem of Auric Plume's reputation system, festival identity, harbor pride, and visible unity.
narrativeUse: moments where spectacle reveals real power
sensoryIdentity: fabric and feather forms moving in sea wind
storyHooks: access to the Roost becomes reputation power
a festival display hides a political move
maintenance routes reveal what the spectacle conceals
a public vow at the Roost changes cargo or harbor legitimacy

beacon.name: Patchwork Hall Beacon
beacon.symbolicRole: Many civic parts joined under one public signal.
beacon.visualRole: Tall blue-lit civic signal rising from Patchwork Hall amid plaza fabric, awnings, kiosks, and harbor-facing streets.
beacon.civicRole: The city's public warning and assembly heart, tying Conjunction signals to civic fabric rather than remote authority.
assetUse: map-background focal point near civic plaza
sensoryIdentity: market noise pausing at signal changes
storyHooks: someone controls the first interpretation of a beacon state
a false public notice causes reputation damage
festival lighting masks a real warning
the plaza becomes too crowded for clean evacuation

seatOfPower.name: Feather Fan Roofs Tide-Seal Citadel
seatOfPower.symbolicRole: Brightness backed by law: display formalized into maritime authority and sealed record.
seatOfPower.visualRole: Central citadel complex of feather fan roofs, tide-seal courts, archive wings, audience terraces, blue lamps, pearl accents, and harbor-facing approaches.
seatOfPower.civicRole: Government center for tide seals, cargo permissions, harbor access, public ceremonies, archives, and maritime rulings.
storyHooks: a tide-seal contradiction threatens a cargo house
public display rights become political leverage
citadel ceremony masks a legal reversal
seal runners expose hidden maintenance routes

majorLandmarks.name: Cargo Yard
majorLandmarks.symbolicRole: Public trust in goods made visible.
majorLandmarks.visualRole: Crates, ropes, cargo ledgers, inventory boards, seal counters, and bright dockside workspaces.
majorLandmarks.civicRole: Manages goods entering and leaving the city and anchors maritime reputation.
storyHooks: a missing cargo seal changes public trust
inventory records contradict visible goods

majorLandmarks.name: Survey Hall
majorLandmarks.symbolicRole: The harbor measured, repaired, and made safe.
majorLandmarks.visualRole: Maps, tables, blue lamps, measuring instruments, permit boards, and civic engineering plazas.
majorLandmarks.civicRole: Controls municipal work, dock repair, harbor route permissions, and infrastructure safety.
storyHooks: a permit delay redirects a crowd
a repair map reveals a hidden route

majorLandmarks.name: Star Observatory
majorLandmarks.visualRole: Observatory tower, celestial court, star instruments, lenses, charts, and high harbor terraces.
majorLandmarks.civicRole: Guides maritime decisions and long-cycle planning through celestial observation.
storyHooks: a star chart is read publicly but disputed privately
navigation data becomes political leverage

majorLandmarks.name: Harbor Basilica
majorLandmarks.symbolicRole: Public vows, maritime mourning, and sacred harbor threshold.
majorLandmarks.visualRole: Pearl domes, water-facing stairs, Harbor Arch, bridgelet, banners, and bright ceremonial stone.
majorLandmarks.civicRole: Hosts ritual arrivals, departures, vows, mourning, and harbor-facing civic ceremonies.
storyHooks: a vow made on the basilica steps becomes binding
a departure ceremony exposes social rank

districtDioramaGuidance: S1 must center the Patchwork Hall Beacon with multi-color awnings, plaza fabric, public kiosks, and showbird citizens using display as civic behavior.
S2 must show visible cargo bureaucracy: Cargo Yard, Inventory Hall, gold seals, crates, ropes, ledger boards, and dock workers.
S3 must read as Public Works Authority through Survey Hall, Permit Yard, maps, measuring tools, blue lamps, and harbor repair markers.
S4 must include Star Observatory, celestial instruments, chart tables, high terraces, and navigation scholars.
S5 must show Harbor Basilica, Harbor Arch, Harbor Bridgelet, pearl-like domes, wet stone, and ritual waterfront space.
mapBackgroundGuidance: Use a bright harbor city with reflective water, fan-roofed civic structures, pearl domes, patchwork awnings, cargo yards, observatory towers, and the Pearl-Feather Roost as skyline anchor.
The Tide-Seal Citadel should occupy central authority space; the Patchwork Hall Beacon should be visible as a civic signal landmark; the Harbor Basilica should anchor the waterfront.
Keep the city colorful and maritime without losing the logistics and public works systems.
elementsToAvoid: empty decorative feathers with no civic function
dull monochrome palette unless used as deliberate contrast
